steps.title: Find the shown leg
steps.content:
From the graph, the shown segment has horizontal change $6$ and vertical change $9$. Its length is
$$\sqrt{6^2+9^2}=\sqrt{117}=3\sqrt{13}$$

steps.title: Use the triangle area
steps.content:
Let $L$ be the other leg. The area of a right triangle is half the product of its legs.
$$\frac{1}{2}(3\sqrt{13})L=48\sqrt{13}$$

steps.title: Solve for the other leg
steps.content:
Multiply by $2$ and divide by $3\sqrt{13}$.
$$L=\frac{96\sqrt{13}}{3\sqrt{13}}=32$$
The answer is B.
